primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury in June 2024
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: Minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
Inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder — CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection administered on 11/05/2024 has no documented treatment duration or number of injections attempted. Payers for CPT 29827 often require documentation of at least one injection trial with a defined observation period before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart addendum specifying the observation period following the 11/05/2024 Kenalog injection and confirm whether a second injection was considered or offered prior to surgical recommendation.

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Typical Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Physical therapy was documented for 8 weeks (September–October 2024). Some BCBS plans require a minimum of 6–12 weeks of formal PT with documented visit counts and progress notes before approving rotator cuff repair under CPT 29827.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Supplement the record with PT visit logs, session counts, and progress notes from September–October 2024 to confirm the full course of therapy and demonstrate documented failure.

denial_risk_flags.label: Attending Surgeon Name Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The attending surgeon is listed as Dr. Michael Torres, MD. Payers require a clearly identified requesting provider for prior authorization; a placeholder token may cause the submission to be rejected or returned as incomplete.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Confirm and populate the attending surgeon's full legal name before submitting the prior authorization request to Blue Cross Blue Shield.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Dates Are Placeholder Tokens
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Both the MRI and X-ray are dated 01/22/2025, which is a placeholder token. Payers require actual imaging dates to verify that imaging was performed within an acceptable timeframe prior to the requested procedure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace 01/22/2025 with the actual imaging date before submission to ensure payer can confirm imaging recency and validity.

denial_risk_flags.label: Surgical Approach Inconsistency — Arthroscopic vs. Mini-Open
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The requested procedure states 'Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair' (CPT 29827), but the surgical approach field documents 'Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair.' A mini-open component may require a different or additional CPT code and could trigger a payer query or denial if the operative plan is not clearly reconciled.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Clarify the operative plan in the chart and confirm whether CPT 29827 alone covers the intended procedure or whether an additional code (e.g., CPT 23412) is needed for the mini-open component.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

diagnosis_codes.note: Two ICD-10 codes are documented (M75.121 and M75.111), clearly supporting the diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear and shoulder impingement.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Three distinct conservative treatments are named: Physical Therapy, Corticosteroid Injection (Kenalog 40mg), and NSAIDs (Meloxicam 15mg daily).

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Physical therapy (8 weeks) and NSAIDs (3 months) have documented durations, but the corticosteroid injection has no documented duration or observation period, leaving one of three treatments incomplete.

imaging_findings.note: Both MRI and X-ray are documented as completed with detailed findings confirming a 2.1 cm full-thickness supraspinatus tear and supporting surgical candidacy.

functional_limitations.note: Four specific functional limitations are explicitly documented, including inability to perform overhead work, disturbed sleep, and inability to return to work duties.

surgical_approach.note: Surgical approach is explicitly documented as 'Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair,' though an inconsistency with the CPT code description is noted.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 (arthroscopic rotator cuff repair) is a valid and appropriate code for the documented diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is explicitly stated as 8 months of failed conservative management, with injury onset documented as June 2024.
